s2.ptxt: Using a plastic hammer, tap into the ring pin.
Install the stud bolts as shown in the illustration.
Using a plastic hammer, tap into the straight pin.
Using a 5 mm hexagon wrench, install the oil nozzles with the bolts.
Using a screwdriver, install a new snap ring at one end of the piston pin hole.

atten4.ptxt: Make sure that the end gap of the snap ring is not aligned with the pin hole cutout portion of the piston.

s2.ptxt: Gradually heat the piston to approximately 80 to 90°C (176 to 194°F).
Align the front marks of the piston and connecting rod, and push in the piston with your thumb.
Using a screwdriver, install a new snap ring on the other end of the piston pin hole.

atten4.ptxt: Make sure that the end gap of the snap ring is not aligned with the pin hole cutout portion of the piston.

s2.ptxt: Check the fitting condition between the piston and piston pin by trying to move the piston back and forth on the piston pin.
Install the oil ring expander and oil ring rail by hand.

item.ptxt: Install the expander and oil ring so that their ring ends are at opposite sides.
Securely install the expander to the inner groove of the oil ring.

s2.ptxt: Using a piston ring expander, install the 2 compression rings so that the paint marks are positioned as shown in the illustration.

atten3.ptxt: Install the compression ring No. 2 with the code mark (2N and 2A) facing upward.

s2.ptxt: Position the piston rings so that the ring ends are as shown in the illustration.
Install the upper bearing with an oil groove on the cylinder block.

atten3.ptxt: Do not apply engine oil to the bearings and the contact surfaces.

s2.ptxt: Install the lower bearing on the bearing cap.

atten3.ptxt: Clean the backside of the bearing and the bearing surface of the connecting rod. The surface should be free of dust and oils.

s2.ptxt: Install the 2 thrust washers under the No. 3 journal of the cylinder block with the oil grooves facing outward.
Apply engine oil to the crankshaft thrust washer.
Apply engine oil to the upper bearing and install the crankshaft on the cylinder block.
Apply engine oil to the lower bearing.
Examine the front marks and install the bearing caps on the cylinder block.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to the threads and under the bearing cap bolts.
Install the crankshaft bearing cap bolts.

atten3.ptxt: The main bearing cap bolts are tightened in 2 progressive steps.

s2.ptxt: Step 1

s3.ptxt: Install and uniformly tighten the 10 main bearing cap bolts in the sequence shown in the illustration.
Retighten the 10 main bearing cap bolts in the sequence shown in the illustration.

s2.ptxt: Step 2

s3.ptxt: Mark the front of the bearing cap bolts with paint.
Retighten the bearing cap bolts by 90°in the numerical order shown in the illustration.

s2.ptxt: Check that the paint mark is now at a 90°angle to the front.
Check that the crankshaft turns smoothly.
Check the crankshaft thrust clearance (See page ).
Align the bearing claw with the groove of the connecting rod or connecting cap.

atten3.ptxt: Do not apply engine oil to the bearings and the contact surfaces.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL PISTON SUB-ASSEMBLY WITH CONNECTING ROD

atten3.ptxt: The connecting rod cap bolts are tightened in 2 progressive steps.

s2.ptxt: Apply engine oil to the cylinder walls, the pistons, and the surfaces of connecting rod bearings.
Check the position of the piston ring ends.
Using a piston ring compressor, push the correctly numbered piston and connecting rod assemblies into each cylinder with the front mark of the piston facing forward.

atten3.ptxt: Match the numbered connecting rod cap with the connecting rod.

s2.ptxt: Check that the protrusion of the connecting rod cap is facing in the correct direction.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to the threads and under the heads of the connecting rod cap bolts.
Install the connecting cap bolts.

atten3.ptxt: The connecting cap bolts should be tightened in 2 progressive steps.

s2.ptxt: Step 1

s3.ptxt: Install and alternately tighten the bolts of the connecting rod cap in several steps.

s2.ptxt: Step 2

s3.ptxt: Mark the front of the connecting rod cap bolts with paint.
Retighten the cap bolts by 90°as shown in the illustration.

s2.ptxt: Check that the crankshaft turns smoothly.
Check the connecting rod thrust clearance (See page ).
Install the bearings in the crankcase and balanceshaft housing.

atten3.ptxt: Do not apply engine oil to the bearings and the contact surfaces.

s2.ptxt: Apply a light coat of engine oil to the bearings.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 1 AND NO. 2 BALANCESHAFT SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Rotate the driven gear No. 1 of balanceshaft No. 1 in the rotating direction until it hits the stopper.

atten3.ptxt: Confirm that the matchmarks on driven gears No. 1 and No. 2 are matched.

s2.ptxt: Align the timing marks of the No. 1 and No. 2 balanceshafts as shown in the illustration.
Place the No. 1 and No. 2 balanceshafts on the crankcase.
Apply a light coat of engine oil under the heads of the balanceshaft housing bolts.
Install the balanceshaft housing bolts.

atten3.ptxt: The balance shaft housing bolts are tightened in 2 progressive steps.

s2.ptxt: Step 1

s3.ptxt: Install and uniformly tighten the 8 bolts in the sequence shown in the illustration.

s2.ptxt: Step 2

s3.ptxt: Mark the front side of each balanceshaft housing bolt head with paint.
Retighten the bolts by 90°as shown in the illustration.
Check that the paint marks are now at a 90°angle to the front.

s2.ptxt: Place a new O-ring on the cylinder block, as shown in the illustration.
Temporarily tighten the pulley set bolt.
Turn the crankshaft to set the crank pins of the No. 1 and No. 4 cylinders to the bottom.

atten4.ptxt: Make sure that the timing mark on the balanceshaft drive gear is positioned as shown in the illustration.

s2.ptxt: Apply seal packing in a continuous bead (diameter: 2.5 to 3.0 mm (0.098 to 0.118 in.)) to the places shown in the illustration.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Black, Three bond 1207B or Equivalent

item.ptxt: Remove any oil from the contact surface.
Install the crankcase within 3 minutes after applying seal packing.
Do not start the engine for at least 2 hours after installing the crankcase stiffener.

s2.ptxt: Install the stiffening crankcase so that the reference holes on the balanceshafts are positioned as shown in the illustration.
Temporarily install the crankcase with the 11 bolts.
Uniformly tighten the 11 bolts in the sequence shown in the illustration.
Wipe off the excess seal packing with a clean piece of cloth.
Turn the crankshaft again to set the key groove to the bottom. Make sure that the timing marks are aligned as shown in the illustration.

atten4.ptxt: An '○' is stamped as a timing mark.

s2.ptxt: Remove the pulley set bolt.
Install a new gasket and oil pump with the 3 bolts.
Using SST and a hammer, evenly tap the oil seal until its surface is flush with the rear oil seal retainer edge.

atten3.ptxt: Keep the lip free from foreign materials.

s2.ptxt: Apply MP grease to a new oil seal lip.

atten3.ptxt: Wipe off extra grease on the crankshaft.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 1 TAPER SCREW PLUG (w/o Oil Cooler)

s2.ptxt: Apply adhesive to 2 or 3 threads of the plug, and install the plug.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Adhesive 1324, Three Bond 1324 or Equivalent

s2.ptxt: Check that no foreign matter is on the mesh part of the filter.
Using an 8 mm socket hexagon wrench, install a new gasket and the oil control valve filter with the screw plug.

atten3.ptxt: Do not touch the mesh when installing the oil control valve filter.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL CYLINDER BLOCK WATER DRAIN COCK SUB-ASSEMBLY (w/o Oil Cooler)

s2.ptxt: Apply adhesive around the drain cock.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Adhesive 1344, Three Bond 1344 or Equivalent

s2.ptxt: Install the water drain cock within the range shown in the illustration.

atten3.ptxt: Do not rotate the drain cocks more than 1 revolution (360°) after tightening the drain cocks to the specified torque.

s2.ptxt: Install the water drain cock plugs to the water drain cocks.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL CYLINDER BLOCK WATER JACKET SPACER

s2.ptxt: Install the water jacket spacer as shown in the illustration.

atten4.ptxt: Be sure to face the slope to the front of the engine.

s2.ptxt: Place a new gasket on the cylinder block surface with the Lot No. stamp facing upward.

item.ptxt: Remove any oil from the contact surface.
Make sure that the gasket is installed in the correct direction.

atten4.ptxt: The cylinder head bolts are tightened in 2 progressive steps.

s2.ptxt: Apply a light coat of engine oil to the bolt threads and the area beneath the bolt heads that come in contact with the washers.
Install the bolts and plate washers to the cylinder head.

atten3.ptxt: Do not drop the washers into the cylinder head.

s2.ptxt: Using several steps, uniformly install and tighten the 10 cylinder head set bolts and plate washers with a 10 mm bi-hexagon wrench in the order shown in the illustration.
Mark the front side of the cylinder head bolt with paint.
Retighten the cylinder head bolts 90° in the sequence shown in the illustration.
Check that the paint mark is now at a 90° angle to the front.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL CAMSHAFT TIMING OIL CONTROL VALVE ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Apply a light coat of engine oil to a new O-ring, then install it onto the camshaft timing oil control valve.
Install the camshaft timing oil control valve with the bolt.
Put the camshaft timing gear and camshaft together with the straight pin and key groove misaligned, as shown in the illustration.
Turn the camshaft timing gear as shown in the illustration while pushing it gently against the camshaft. Push further at the position where the pin fits into the groove.

atten3.ptxt: Be sure not to turn the camshaft timing gear to the retard angle side (the right angle).

s2.ptxt: Check that there is no clearance between the gear and camshaft.
Tighten the flange bolt with the camshaft timing gear fixed in place.
Check that the camshaft timing gear can move to the retard angle side (the right direction) and is locked in the most retarded position.
Clamp the camshaft in a vise.
Tighten the flange bolt with the camshaft timing sprocket fixed.
Install the No. 1 camshaft bearing.
Install the No. 2 camshaft bearing.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to the journal portion of the camshaft.
Examine the front marks and numbers, and check that the order is as shown in the illustration. Then install the bearing caps into the cylinder head.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to the threads and under the heads of the bearing cap bolts.
Using several steps, uniformly tighten the 10 bearing cap bolts in the sequence shown in the illustration.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to the journal portion of the No. 2 camshaft.
Examine the front marks and numbers, and check that the order is as shown in the illustration. Then install the bearing caps onto the cylinder head.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to the threads and under the heads of the bearing cap bolts.
Using several steps, uniformly tighten the 10 bearing cap bolts in the sequence shown in the illustration.
Install the 2 keys.
Set the crankshaft key into the left horizontal position.
Turn the drive shaft so that the cutout faces upward.
Align the yellow mark links with the timing marks of each gear as shown in the illustration.
Install the sprockets onto the crankshaft and oil pump shaft with the chain wrapped on the gears.
Temporarily tighten the oil pump drive shaft sprocket with the nut.
Insert the damper spring into the adjusting hole, and then install the chain tensioner plate with the bolt.
Align the adjusting hole of the oil pump drive shaft sprocket with the groove of the oil pump.
Insert a 4 mm diameter bar into the adjusting hole of the oil pump drive shaft gear to lock the gear in position, and then tighten the nut.
Rotate the crankshaft clockwise by 90°, and align the crankshaft key to the top.
Install the crankshaft timing sprocket.
Install the chain vibration damper with the 2 bolts.
Set the No. 1 cylinder to TDC/compression.

s3.ptxt: Turn the camshafts with a wrench (using the hexagonal lobe) to align the timing marks of the camshaft timing gear with each timing mark located on the No. 1 and No. 2 bearing caps as shown in the illustration.
Using the crankshaft pulley bolt, turn the crankshaft to position with the key on the crankshaft upward.

s2.ptxt: Install the chain onto the crankshaft timing sprocket with the gold or pink mark link aligned with the timing mark on the crankshaft.
Using SST and a hammer, tap in the crankshaft timing sprocket.
Align the gold or yellow link with each timing mark located on the camshaft timing gear and sprocket, then install the chain.
Install the chain tensioner slipper with the bolt.
Install the timing chain guide with the bolt.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 1 CRANKSHAFT POSITION SENSOR PLATE

s2.ptxt: Install the sensor plate with the "F" mark facing forward.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL TIMING CHAIN CASE OIL SEAL

s2.ptxt: Using SST, tap in a new oil seal until its surface is flush with the timing chain cover edge.
Apply a light coat of MP grease to the lip of the oil seal.

atten3.ptxt: Keep the gap between the timing chain cover edge and the oil seal free of foreign matter.

s2.ptxt: Remove any old packing (FIPG) material and be careful not to drop any oil on the contact surfaces of the timing chain cover, cylinder head and cylinder block.
Apply seal packing (diameter: 4.0 to 4.5 mm (0.157 to 0.177 in.)) as shown in the illustration.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Black, Three Bond 1207B or Equivalent

s2.ptxt: Apply seal packing in a continuous bead as shown in the illustration.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Black, Three Bond 1207B or Equivalent

item.ptxt: Remove any oil from the contact surface.
Install the chain cover within 3 minutes after applying seal packing.
Do not start the engine for at least 2 hours after installing.

s2.ptxt: Install the timing chain cover with the 12 bolts and 2 nuts.

entry.ptxt: 30 mm (1.18 in.) length for 10 mm head
30 mm (1.18 in.) length for 12 mm head
40 mm (1.57 in.) length for 14 mm head

s2.ptxt: Using a E10 "torx" socket, install the stud bolt to the drive belt tensioner.
Install the V-ribbed belt tensioner with the bolt and nut.

atten3.ptxt: Do not lift the engine more than necessary.

s2.ptxt: Remove any old packing material and be careful not to drop any oil on the contact surfaces of the cylinder block and oil pan.
Apply a continuous bead of seal packing (Diameter 3.0 to 4.0 mm (0.118 to 0.157 in.)) as shown in the illustration.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Block, Three Bond 1207B or Equivalent

item.ptxt: Remove any oil from the contact surfaces.
Install the oil pan within 3 minutes after applying seal packing.
Do not start the engine for at least 2 hours after installing.

s2.ptxt: Install the oil pan to the cylinder block.
Uniformly tighten the 12 bolts and 2 nuts in the sequence shown in the illustration.
Install a new gasket and oil pan drain plug with a new gasket.
Remove any old seal packing material from the contact surface.
Apply a continuous line of seal packing as shown in the illustration.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Black, Three Bond 1207B or Equivalent

item.ptxt: Remove any oil from the contact surface.
The parts must be set within 3 minutes after applying seal packing. Otherwise, the material must be removed and reapplied.

s2.ptxt: Install the water pump and clamp bracket with the 4 bolts and 2 nuts.
Install the wire of the crankshaft position sensor onto the clamp bracket.
Install the clamp of the crankshaft position sensor onto the water pump.
Using SST, install the water pump pulley with the 4 bolts.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to the O-ring of the sensor.
Confirm that the wire harness of the sensor is placed as shown in the illustration.
Install the sensor with the 2 bolts.
Install the clamp of the crankshaft position sensor onto the water pump.
TMMK made:

s3.ptxt: Align the pulley set key with the key groove of the pulley.
Using SST, fix the pulley in place and tighten the bolt.

s2.ptxt: TMC made:

s3.ptxt: Align the pulley set key with the key groove of the pulley.
Using SST, fix the pulley in place and tighten the bolt.

s2.ptxt: Release the ratchet pawl, then fully push in the plunger and hook the hook to the pin so that the plunger is in the position shown in the illustration.
Install a new gasket and the chain tensioner with the 2 nuts.

atten3.ptxt: If the hook releases the plunger while the chain tensioner is being installed, set the hook again.

s2.ptxt: Turn the crankshaft counterclockwise, then disconnect the plunger knock pin from the hook.
Turn the crankshaft clockwise, then check that the plunger is extended.
Check only the valves indicated.

s3.ptxt: Using a feeler gauge, measure the clearance between the valve lifter and camshaft.
Record any out-of-specification valve clearance measurements. They will be used later to determine the required replacement valve clearance lifters.

s2.ptxt: Turn the crankshaft 1 revolution (360°) and set the No. 4 cylinder to the TDC/compression.
Check only the valves indicated.

s3.ptxt: Using a feeler gauge, measure the clearance between the valve lifter and camshaft.
Record any out-of-specification valve clearance measurements. They will be used later to determine the required replacement valve lifters.

s2.ptxt: Remove the No. 2 camshaft (see page ).
Remove the camshaft (see page ).
Remove the valve lifters.
Using a micrometer, measure the thickness of the removed valve lifters.
Calculate the thickness of a new lifter so that the valve clearance comes within the specified values.
CALCULATION EXAMPLE (Intake):

item.ptxt: Measured intake valve clearance = 0.40 mm (0.0158 in.)
(Measured - Specification = Excess clearance)
Measured used lifter measurement = 5.250 mm (0.2067 in.)
New lifter thickness = 5.410 mm (0.2130 in.)
(Excess clearance + Used lifter thickness = Ideal new lifter)
Closest new lifter = 5.420 mm (0.2134 in.)
Select No. 42 lifter

s2.ptxt: Select a new lifter with a thickness as close as possible to the calculated values.

item.ptxt: Lifters are available in 35 sizes in increments of 0.020 mm (0.0008 in.), from 5.060 to 5.740 mm (0.1992 to 0.2260 in.).
The identification number inside the valve lifters shows the value to 2 decimal places. (The illustration shows 5.420 mm (0.2134 in.)

s2.ptxt: Valve lifter selection chart (intake).
EXAMPLE:
The 5.250 mm (0.2067 in.) lifter is installed, and the measured clearance is 0.400 mm (0.0157 in.). Replace the 5.250 mm (0.2067 in.) lifter with a new No. 42 lifter.
Valve lifter selection chart (exhaust).
EXAMPLE:
The 5.340 mm (0.2102 in.) lifter is installed, and the measured clearance is 0.430 mm (0.0169 in.). Replace the 5.340 mm (0.2102 in.) lifter with a new No. 42 lifter.
Install the selected valve lifter.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to the O-ring of the sensor.
Install the sensor with the bolt.
Install the gasket to the cylinder head cover.

atten3.ptxt: Remove any oil from the contact surface.

s2.ptxt: Remove any old packing material from the contact surface.
Apply seal packing to the 2 locations shown in the illustration.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Block, Three Bond 1207B or Equivalent

item.ptxt: Remove any oil from the contact surface.
Install the cylinder head cover within 3 minutes of applying seal packing.
Do not add engine oil for at least 2 hours after installing the cylinder head cover.

s2.ptxt: Install the cylinder head cover with the 8 bolts and 2 nuts.
Install the 2 engine wires with the 2 bolts.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL OIL FILTER UNION (w/o Oil Cooler)

s2.ptxt: Using a 12 mm hexagon wrench, install the oil filter union.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL OIL COOLER ASSEMBLY (w/ Oil Cooler)

s2.ptxt: Clean the oil cooler contact surface on the cooler mounting.
Install a new O-ring to the oil cooler.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to the threads and under the head of the union bolt.
Install the oil cooler with the plate washer, oil filter union and nut.
Apply adhesive to the threads of the oil filter union.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Adhesive 1324, Three Bond 1324 or Equivalent

s2.ptxt: Install the union bolt.
Check and clean the oil filter installation surface.
Apply clean engine oil to the gasket of a new oil filter.
Lightly screw the oil filter into place, and tighten it until the gasket contacts the seat.
When using a torque wrench:

s3.ptxt: Using SST, tighten the oil filter.

s2.ptxt: When not using a torque wrench:

s3.ptxt: Using SST, tighten it an additional 3/4 turn.

s2.ptxt: Install the spark plugs.
Apply adhesive to the threads of the ventilation valve.
Install the ventilation valve.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Adhesive 1324, Three Bond 1324 or Equivalent

s2.ptxt: Install the gasket to the cap.
Install the oil filler cap.
